User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the application, capable of submitting projects and (for admins) moderating them.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for authentication.

nickname.description: The user's chosen nickname, displayed on project cards.

role.description: The user's role within the application (e.g., 'user', 'admin').

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a project submitted by a student for the 'GDGOC Unilorin Build With AI Demo Board' showcase.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Project entity.

shortDescription.description: A brief summary of the project, displayed on project cards.

fullDescription.description: The comprehensive description of the project.

screenshotUrl.description: URL to the project's main screenshot or image.

liveUrl.description: URL to the live deployed version of the project.

githubUrl.description: URL to the project's GitHub repository.

status.description: The current moderation status of the project (e.g., 'pending', 'approved', 'rejected').

submissionDate.description: Timestamp when the project was submitted.

approvedDate.description: Timestamp when the project was approved, if applicable.

reviewerId.description: Reference to the User who approved or rejected the project. (Relationship: User 1:N Project)

userId.description: Reference to the User who submitted this project. (Relationship: User 1:N Project)

aiToolIds.description: References to the AI Tools used in this project. (Relationship: AITool N:N Project)

techStackIds.description: References to the Tech Stack items used in this project. (Relationship: TechStack N:N Project)

batchId.description: Reference to the Batch this project belongs to. (Relationship: Batch 1:N Project)

AITool.title: AITool
AITool.description: Represents a specific AI tool or technology utilized in projects.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AI Tool entity.

name.description: The name of the AI tool (e.g., 'TensorFlow', 'ChatGPT API').

description.description: An optional description of the AI tool.

TechStack.title: TechStack
TechStack.description: Represents a technology or framework used in projects.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Tech Stack entity.

name.description: The name of the technology or framework (e.g., 'React', 'Python', 'MongoDB').

description.description: An optional description of the technology.

Batch.title: Batch
Batch.description: Represents a specific batch or cohort of the 'Build With AI' program.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Batch entity.

startDate.description: The start date of the batch.

endDate.description: The end date of the batch.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Access is restricted to the user themselves (for read/update) and administrators (for read). Includes `userId` (document ID) for path-based authorization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for admin role management. The existence of a document with `adminId` matching `request.auth.uid` grants admin privileges. Authorization relies on `exists()` checks, ensuring independence.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique identifier of an administrator, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Contains all submitted projects (pending, approved, rejected). Includes denormalized `userId` (renamed to `ownerId` for clarity in rules) for ownership authorization and `status` for public/private visibility control. Both are critical for Authorization Independence and QAPs.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the project.

definition.description: Globally accessible catalog of AI tools. Publicly readable.

params.name: aiToolId
params.description: The unique identifier of an AI tool.

definition.description: Globally accessible catalog of tech stack items. Publicly readable.

params.name: techStackId
params.description: The unique identifier of a tech stack item.

definition.description: Globally accessible catalog of program batches. Publicly readable.

params.name: batchId
params.description: The unique identifier of a batch.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to adhere strictly to the CORE DESIGN PRINCIPLES and DESIGN STRATEGY MANDATES, prioritizing authorization independence, clarity, and scalability. 

**Authorization Independence:**
1.  **Users (`/users/{userId}`):** User profiles are secured by path-based ownership. The `userId` in the path directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid`, ensuring that rules can validate ownership without needing `get()` operations on other documents. This allows users to read/update their own profiles atomically.
2.  **Projects (`/projects/{projectId}`):** This collection is central to the application's data flow and is designed with critical denormalization:
    *   The `userId` (submitter's ID) is denormalized directly onto each `Project` document as `ownerId`. This allows project owners to manage their own submissions (e.g., edit a 'pending' project) by comparing `request.auth.uid` with `resource.data.ownerId` directly on the `Project` document, eliminating `get()` calls to the `/users` collection for basic ownership checks.
    *   The `status` field (`pending`, `approved`, `rejected`) is also on the `Project` document. Public read access to projects is granted *only* if `resource.data.status == 'approved'`. This check is performed directly on the requested document, preventing `get()` calls to infer visibility.
3.  **Admin Roles (`/roles_admin/{adminId}`):** Admin status is determined by the *existence* of a document in this collection where the document ID matches the `request.auth.uid`. Firebase Security Rules can use the `exists()` function to check for admin privileges. This is highly performant and doesn't require reading the full `User` document, thus maintaining authorization independence and supporting DBAC (Database-Backed Access Control) via existence over content.
4.  **Static Data (`/ai_tools`, `/tech_stack`, `/batches`):** These collections are publicly readable and do not require any user-specific authorization or `get()` calls for access.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) Support:**
1.  **Homepage Project Board (`/projects` list):** The structure allows for secure `list` operations for the homepage. Rules will enforce that `list` queries for public users *must* include a filter for `status == 'approved'`. If a query attempts to list projects without this filter, or for a different status, the rule will deny it, ensuring that only approved projects are retrieved by unauthorized users (QAP compliant, as the rule actively denies non-compliant requests).
2.  **User's Submitted Projects (`/projects` list for owner):** Authenticated users can query for their own projects by filtering `ownerId == request.auth.uid`. The rules will ensure that only projects matching the requesting user's ID are returned, or access is denied if the filter is missing or incorrect.
3.  **Admin Moderation Dashboard (`/projects` list for admin):** Administrators, whose `uid` exists in `/roles_admin`, will be granted `list` access to the entire `/projects` collection. This allows them to query and view projects of all statuses without requiring specific filters, directly supporting the Admin Moderation Page requirement and adhering to QAPs for privileged users.

**Structural Segregation:**
*   **Users:** `/users/{userId}` for user-specific data.
*   **Projects:** A single `/projects/{projectId}` collection holds all projects (pending, approved, rejected). This simplifies admin moderation (a single collection to list from) and project submission, while robust security rules leveraging `status` and `ownerId` manage public, owner, and admin access without requiring separate collections for different states (e.g., no `/public_projects`).
*   **Static Lookups:** Dedicated top-level collections like `/ai_tools`, `/tech_stack`, `/batches` provide clear, public read-only access to global lookup data.
*   **Roles:** A dedicated `/roles_admin/{adminId}` collection is used solely for robust admin role checking.
